steps.title: Use the context for conceivable
steps.content: Seacole says nature seemed determined to throw every conceivable obstacle in the travelers' way. In that context, conceivable means able to be imagined or thought of, as in every obstacle that could possibly exist.

steps.title: Reject mismatched meanings
steps.content: Choice A means able to be seen, but the word concerns what can be imagined. Choice B means steady, which does not fit obstacles. Choice C means not difficult, contradicting the passage's description of the journey. The answer is D.
